person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Really about the only accurate thing published about Edward Morrison can be found in the excellent article, "The Heath Connection: English Origins of Isaac and William Heath of Roxbury, Massachusetts, John Johnson, Edward Morris, and Elizabeth (Morris) Cartwright," The New England Historical and Genealogical Register, Vol 146. Boston, MA: New England Historic Genealogical Society, 1995, page 266. See especially starting on page 277.  This article describes how the author came upon the marriage record for Edward Morrison and Prudence Heath, and all the reasons behind why it is highly likely that Edward and Prudence are the parents of immigrants Edward Morris and his sister Elizabeth Morris.  

According to this article, no christening record has yet been found for Edward Morrison.  He was probably born about 1595, but we don't know exactly when or where.  Edward was a bargeman, and he married Prudence Heath at St. Mary Mounthaw, London, 25 October 1622.  St Mary Mounthaw or Mounthaut was a parish church in Old Fish Street Hill in the City of London. Of medieval origin, it was destroyed in the Great Fire of London in 1666 and not rebuilt.

His children were christened in Great Amwell in Hertfordshire, about 20 miles north of London.  This couple had five known children in Great Amwell; their family life was cut short by Edward's burial at Great Amwell on June 22, 1631. Known children: 

Elizabeth bp at Great Amwell 11 Feb 1623/4;m Edward Cartwright
Isaac bp at Ware 03 Feb 1625/6; nothing further
Son buried at Ware 28 Oct 1627
Mary bp at Great Amwell 10 Dec 1629;nothing further
Edward bp at Great Amwell 22 Jan 1631/2; m Grace Bett

Daughter Elizabeth and son Edward lived as adults in the Massachusetts Bay Colony.  They likely came as young adults, perhaps servants.  Elizabeth and Edward were not the only people from the parish of Great Amwell to move to New England.  Richard Warren (d. 1628), a passenger on the Mayflower in 1620, who settled in Plymouth Colony and co-signed the Mayflower Compact, was married on 14 April 1610 at Great Amwell to Elizabeth Walker, daughter of Augustine Walker.

The parish church in Great Amwell is a medieval building dedicated to St John the Baptist. The nave and chancel date from the 11th century with a Norman chancel arch. 

Although Edward's daughter lived to be an adult and was married, her husband apparently died at sea only a few years later and there is no record of any children.  The only known descendants of Edward are through his son Edward.

sourceList.title: Edward Morrison in entry for Susan Morrison, "England, Middlesex Parish Registers, 1539-1988"

zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Morrison於1595年出生在英國時，他的父親Edward Morris II30歲，母親Lady Elizabeth Catherine Norreys24歲。他於1622年10月25日與Prudence Heath在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 瑪麗 薩默塞特 煙雨 聖 瑪麗 莫塔福結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他在英國英格蘭賀特福爾被列為bargeman。他去世於1632年在英國英格蘭享年37歲，埋葬在英國賀特福爾格亞特 阿維爾。

nameMeanings.name: Morrison
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: from Ó Muirgheasáin ‘descendant of Muirgheasán’, a diminutive of Muirgheas, a personal name meaning ‘sea valor’.
Scottish: from Gaelic Mac Moireasdain ‘son of Moireasdan’, a personal name cognate with Irish Muirgheasán’ (see 1 above).
Scottish and English: patronymic from the personal name   Morris  .

variants.name: Morrisson
meanings: English: variant of   Morrison  .

variants.name: Morson
meanings: English, Scottish and Irish: shortened form of   Morrison  .
English: sometimes a variant of Moorsom, a habitational name from Moorsholm in North Yorkshire. The placename derives from Old English mōr ‘moor’ + hūsum, dative plural form of hūs ‘house’.

variants.name: Morison
meanings: Scottish (northern): variant of   Morrison  .

variants.name: Morris
meanings: English and Scottish: from Maurice, an Old French personal name introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from Latin Mauritius, a derivative of Maurus (see Moore ). This was the name of several early Christian saints. In some cases it may be a nickname of the same derivation for someone with a swarthy complexion.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Muirghis, a variant of Ó Muirgheasa (see Morrissey ).

Welsh: Anglicized form of the personal name Meurig (from Latin Mauritius), which was gradually superseded in Wales by Morus, Morys, a derivative of the Anglo-Norman French form of the name (see 1 above).

variants.name: McCorriston
meanings: Scottish (Wigtownshire): probably from Mac Thoireasdain ‘son of Tòireasdan or Tòrasdan’, a Gaelicized form of the Old Norse name Thórrsteinn ‘thunder stone’. It may also be from a lenited form of Gaelic Mac Moireasdain ‘son of Moireasdan’; see Morrison .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Morrison was born in 1595, in England, his father, Edward Morris II, was 30 and his mother, Lady Elizabeth Catherine Norreys, was 24. He married Prudence Heath on 25 October 1622, in St Mary Somerset with St Mary Mounthaw, London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. His occupation is listed as bargeman in Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom. He died in 1632, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 37, and was buried in Great Amwell, Hertfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Morrison est né en 1595, à Angleterre, son père, Edward Morris II, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Lady Elizabeth Catherine Norreys, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Prudence Heath le 25 octobre 1622, à St Mary Somerset with St Mary Mounthaw, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est bargeman à Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé en 1632, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 37 ans, et a été enterré à Great Amwell, Hertfordshire, Angleterre.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Morrison è nato il nel 1595 nel England, il suo padre, Edward Morris II, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Lady Elizabeth Catherine Norreys, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Prudence Heath il 25 ottobre 1622, nel St Mary Somerset with St Mary Mounthaw, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come bargeman nel Hertfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì nel 1632, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 37 anni, e fu sepolto a Great Amwell, Hertfordshire, England.

ja.Great Amwell, Hertfordshire, England, Un: イギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアグレアト アムウエル
ja.lifeSummary: Edward Morrisonが1595年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Edward Morris IIは30歳，母Lady Elizabeth Catherine Norreysは24歳であった。1622年10月25日にイギリスイギリスロンドンスト マリー ソメルセト ウィス スト マリー モウンサウでPrudence Heathと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。イギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアでの職業はbargemanと記録されている。彼は1632年にイギリスイギリスで37歳で死去し，イギリスハートフォードシュアイアグレアト アムウエルにて埋葬された。

ko.St Mary Mounthaw, London, England: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 메리 서머세트 위스 스트 메리 모우스아w
ko.lifeSummary: Edward Morrison(이)가 1595년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward Morris II(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Elizabeth Catherine Norreys(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1622년 10월 25일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 메리 서머세트 위스 스트 메리 모우스아w에서 Prudence Heath(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르에서 bargeman이었다. 그는 1632년 영국 잉글랜드에서 37세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허트퍼드실르 그리트 아웰에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Morrison nasceu em 1595, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Edward Morris II, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Lady Elizabeth Catherine Norreys, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Prudence Heath em 25 de outubro de 1622, em St Mary Somerset with St Mary Mounthaw, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como bargeman em Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 1632, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 37 anos, e foi sepultado em Great Amwell, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.St Mary Mounthaw, London, England: ст мэри сомирсет вит ст мэри маунтав, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Morrison родился в 1595 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Edward Morris II было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Elizabeth Catherine Norreys, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Prudence Heath 25 октября 1622 г. в местности ст мэри сомирсет вит ст мэри маунтав, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери.  Он работал по профессии bargeman в местности хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер в 1632 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 37 лет и был похоронен в местности грит амвелл, хиртфордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Morrison nació en 1595, en Inglaterra, su papá, Edward Morris II, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Lady Elizabeth Catherine Norreys, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Prudence Heath el 25 de octubre de 1622, en St Mary Somerset with St Mary Mounthaw, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era bargeman en Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió en 1632, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 37 años, y fue sepultado en Great Amwell, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.
